checks.a_analysis: The output faithfully represents the paragraphs and sentences found in the source document. No fabricated body text or invented sections were identified. The structure follows the numbered and lettered sections of the original minutes accurately.

A URL was identified in the output that does not exist as text within the source PDF. In Section 6.b (State Government Users Group Committee), the word 'document' in the second paragraph is hyperlinked to 'https://files.nc.gov/ncdit/GICC-TAC-OpenSource-GIS-Software-20171201.pdf'. In the source document, this word is a simple verb and is not hyperlinked. Furthermore, the source document contains a hyperlink on the word 'document' in Section 6.e (Technical Advisory Committee), but the actual URL text is not visible in the document. The inclusion of an explicit URL string in the output that is not present in the source text, especially when attached to the incorrect instance of the word in a different section, constitutes a fabrication/misplacement of contact-type information.
checks.b_evidence_source: John will work with Tim to document accomplishments from the previous fiscal year for the GICC annual report.
checks.b_evidence_output: John will work with Tim to [document](https://files.nc.gov/ncdit/GICC-TAC-OpenSource-GIS-Software-20171201.pdf) accomplishments from the previous fiscal year for the GICC annual report.

checks.a_analysis: All pages and sections (1 through 9, including subsections a-e under section 6) are present in the output. No content from the source was omitted.
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All sections contain the full text present in the source. No significant blocks of text or key sentences were omitted from any section.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Minor differences such as the normalization of '8 points/m^2' to '8 points/m2' and whitespace corrections are acceptable reformatting. No wording changes that alter the meaning of the document were found.

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated text blocks from the source. Page-level headers present in the OCR (e.g., 'GICC M&O Minutes 2') have been correctly omitted or filtered, and no paragraphs or headings appear more than once.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. The document title is captured in metadata. Major numbered sections (1-9) are assigned H2, and sub-points (a, b, c...) are assigned H3. There are no skips in heading levels (e.g., H1 to H3).
The source document does not contain any tables; therefore, no table structure corruption is possible.
The reading order follows the logical flow of the original document. Text from multiple pages is correctly merged into a continuous stream, and section headings precede their respective body content.

The document's primary numbered and lettered items are converted into a heading hierarchy (H2 and H3). This is an acceptable structural transformation for accessibility that preserves the meaning and relationship of the items.

The link intended for the word 'document' in section 6e (Technical Advisory Committee) on page 3 was incorrectly injected into the word 'document' in section 6b (State Government Users Group Committee) on page 2. The original word in section 6e is no longer linked.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 3, Section 6e: 'An item to work on is an update to the open source document to include...' (The word 'document' is a hyperlink).
checks.b_evidence_output: Page 2 equivalent, Section 6b: 'John will work with Tim to document accomplishments from the previous fiscal year...' (The word 'document' is hyperlinked in the output).
checks.b_evidence_location: Section '6. Status Reports', subsection 'b. State Government Users Group Committee'

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly reflects the single-column visual organization of the source PDF. The sequence of headings (1 through 9) and sub-headings (6a-e, 8a-b) is maintained. The transition of Section 5 across PDF pages 1 and 2 is reconciled into a single continuous section in the HTML. The addition of a Table of Contents and the placement of the document title as a main page header are appropriate for a web rendering and do not disrupt the logical flow of information.
There are no tables present in the source PDF or the rendered HTML output.
The source PDF is a text-only document and does not contain any meaningful images, logos, or charts. Consequently, no images are required in the HTML output.

The body content paragraphs from the PDF are accurately represented once in the HTML output. While the document title and header information appear both at the top of the page (as a title/header) and within the content body (under the 'MINUTES' heading), this is consistent with standard accessible web design practices and the provided instructions, which state that titles appearing in both navigation/header and body areas are not considered duplication. No genuine body paragraphs or sections are repeated.
